title: Fermented Light Aussie Wonton Dumplings
description: A light, fermented dumpling dish using glutenfree wonton skins, packed with fresh cucumber, carrot, and microgreens, finished in a pressure cooker for a quick solo dinner.
instructions: Rinse the wonton skins under cold water and pat dry; set aside.
In a bowl, combine kimchi, cucumber, carrot, micro‑greens, tamari, rice vinegar, sesame oil, lime juice, salt, and pepper. Mix well to form the filling.
Place a teaspoon of filling in the center of each wonton skin, leaving a 1‑inch border.
Fold the skins into triangular shapes and seal the edges by pinching and pressing firmly; repeat until all skins are filled.
Place the dumplings in a single layer in the pressure cooker’s steamer basket or on a heat‑proof rack.
Add 1 cup of water to the pressure cooker, secure the lid, and cook on high pressure for 5 minutes.
Quick‑release the pressure, remove the dumplings, and serve immediately with a squeeze of lime or a drizzle of soy sauce if desired.
